meta.name: BIS-15 Barratt Impulsiveness Scale
meta.description: The BIS-15 is a 15-item scale measuring three dimensions of impulsivity: Attentional (difficulty focusing), Motor (acting without thinking), and Non-planning (lack of future orientation).
meta.author: Spinella (2007)
meta.reference: Spinella M. Normative data and a short form of the Barratt Impulsiveness Scale. International Journal of Neuroscience. 2007;117(3):359-368.
meta.instruction: Please rate how often each statement applies to you.

questions.text: I make up my mind quickly
I act on the spur of the moment
I answer without thinking when I don't know the answer
I don't plan for the future
I am more interested in the present than the future
I find it hard to think about complex problems

ranges.label: Low Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity level is low. You generally think things through before acting.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your thoughtful and planned approach to decisions
Being cautious is positive, but also allow some flexibility in adapting to changes

ranges.label: Moderate Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity is within the normal range. You maintain a good balance between thinking and acting.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your good self-regulation skills
Give yourself extra time to think before making important decisions
Keep practicing focus and planning skills

ranges.label: Elevated Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity is somewhat elevated. You may sometimes act without fully considering the consequences.
suggestions: Try pausing for a few seconds before making decisions
Use to-do lists and schedules to enhance planning
Practice mindfulness meditation to improve self-awareness
If impulsive behavior causes problems, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: High Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity level is high. You may frequently act without thinking, which can affect work, relationships, and daily life.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional for evaluation
Learn delayed gratification and self-control techniques
Establish daily routines and structured lifestyle habits
Consider cognitive behavioral therapy to manage impulsive behavior
Explore whether impulsive behavior may be related to ADHD or other conditions

attentional.label: Low
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is low. You have good ability to concentrate.

attentional.label: Moderate
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is at a moderate level. You may sometimes have difficulty focusing.

attentional.label: High
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is elevated. You may frequently have difficulty concentrating.

attentional.label: Very High
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is very high. Significant difficulty with concentration; further evaluation recommended.

motor.label: Low
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is low. Your actions are generally well-considered.

motor.label: Moderate
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is at a moderate level. You occasionally act without thinking.

motor.label: High
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is elevated. Impulsive actions occur frequently.

motor.label: Very High
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is very high. You frequently act without thinking; consider seeking professional help.

nonplanning.label: Low
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is low. You have good planning and foresight abilities.

nonplanning.label: Moderate
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is at a moderate level. You sometimes lack long-term planning.

nonplanning.label: High
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is elevated. You rarely plan ahead for the future.

nonplanning.label: Very High
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is very high. You almost never plan ahead; consider developing planning habits.
